meta.title: Conversation Sync Proof Spine
meta.description: A Lev runtime design brief for replacing DONE strings and pane scraping with session evidence, FlowMind FSM state, gate receipts, and intent-conservation proof.

props.title: Conversation Sync Proof Spine
props.subtitle: Close is an FSM state. Receipts are evidence. Gates decide.

props.content: The core move is not to add another lifecycle system. Lev already has loops, gates, receipts, FlowMind, and proof artifacts. This design binds them into one spine: every agent turn produces session evidence, every meaningful step emits scoped receipts, every completion claim is resolved by gates, and close becomes a state transition backed by proof references.

props.label: Proof State
props.title: Branch Resolution Snapshot
props.subtitle: The current design branches are resolved enough to move into proposal and dogfood execution.

trend.value: close, stack, receipts, verification, scope, classifiers, core envelope
needs prototype proof, not more naming debate
receipts remain evidence; gates remain authority

props.label: Current vs Ideal
props.title: What Changes
props.subtitle: The target is an integration of existing Lev primitives, not a parallel system.

props.label: CURRENT
props.title: String and pane driven lifecycle
props.content: Some flows still infer lifecycle from DONE text, tmux pane scraping, manual nudges, or loose closeout conventions. Prompt-stack proves the value of sequenced prompting, but it needs constant supervision because step completion and proof submission are not first-class enough yet.

props.label: IDEAL
props.title: Evidence and gate driven lifecycle
props.content: FlowMind owns the step/FSM state. Provider JSONL and session stores prove the turn happened. Scoped receipts record observations and step claims. Gate receipts decide whether the claim advances. Close is just the terminal FSM state once proof refs satisfy policy.

props.label: Runtime
props.title: Truth Flow
props.subtitle: The actual work happens between propose and gate; this flow makes that work observable and replayable.

props.title: Durable dispatch
props.content: FlowMind emits the next step request with run envelope, task intent, step id, and active policy.

props.title: Session evidence
props.content: tmux controls process isolation and input injection; JSONL/session stores prove response boundaries.

props.title: Step receipt
props.content: A scoped receipt records observed response, bound classifier variables, and step-local evidence.

props.title: Gate receipt
props.content: The relevant gate evaluates evidence: intent conserved, proof sufficient, next state legal.

props.title: Close FSM
props.content: Close consumes refs and transitions to closed, reaper, learning, handoff, or the next FlowMind step.

props.label: Authority
props.title: Receipts Do Not Certify Themselves
props.subtitle: This is the key inflation guard: more receipts should mean more evidence, not more authority.

props.title: Receipt role
props.content: Receipts record structured observations: response seen, step output captured, classifier values bound, trace ids attached, artifacts linked. A receipt can say what happened; it cannot prove the work is good by itself.

props.title: Gate role
props.content: Gates consume receipts and external evidence to make state-changing decisions. Intent conservation, proof sufficiency, adversarial review, and close policy all stay gate-owned.

props.label: Core Loop
props.title: Intent Conservation in the Loop
props.subtitle: The core loop stays plan-act-verify; FlowMind overlays it with step policy and domain classifiers.

props.caption: FlowMind overlays the loop, but cannot replace the proof boundary.
props.content:
flowchart LR
  A[Seed intent] --> B[Plan]
  B --> C[Act candidate effect]
  C --> D[Observe diff and transcript]
  D --> E[Classify evidence]
  E --> F{Intent conservation gate}
  F -->|conserved| G[Keep tick]
  F -->|unclear| H[Hold and probe]
  F -->|drifted| I[Mutate next tick]
  F -->|violated| J[Discard or escalate]
  G --> K{Close predicate}
  K -->|refs satisfy policy| L[Closed]
  K -->|proof missing| H
  H --> B
  I --> B

One Lev loop is not merely the model saying done. Operationally, a loop/tick is the smallest state-changing pass that can produce evidence, be gated, and either advance, hold, mutate, discard, or close. That maps cleanly to the OOO-style tick insight without importing its name into Lev canon.

props.label: Ledger
props.title: Branch Resolution Ledger
props.subtitle: Each branch has a decision, a proof source, and a fitness function that prevents the design from drifting back into pane scraping.

props.title: Close authority
props.content:
**Decision:** Close is an FSM state, not a receipt family.

**Proof gate:** close rejects pane DONE and requires gate refs.

props.title: Prompt-stack supersession
props.content:
**Decision:** Prompt-stack remains POC protocol until FlowMind reaches parity.

**Proof gate:** FlowMind replays prompt-stack fixtures before retirement.

props.title: Receipt authority
props.content:
**Decision:** Receipts are evidence; gates own advancement.

**Proof gate:** no receipt can certify itself.

props.title: Verification mode
props.content:
**Decision:** Self-verification is allowed only for low-risk steps; adversarial review is required for high-risk gates.

**Proof gate:** self mode cannot close high-risk flows.

props.title: Scoped events
props.content:
**Decision:** Sessions, receipts, and event buses can be scoped and projected without becoming gate authority.

**Proof gate:** scope refs are required; projections cannot advance state.

props.title: Classifier variables
props.content:
**Decision:** Variables like user.mood are bound from classifier output triples.

**Proof gate:** vars require value, confidence, and evidence_count.

props.title: Core envelope
props.content:
**Decision:** Every mutating flow inherits the core run envelope and intent-conservation gate.

**Proof gate:** SDLC and non-SDLC overlays cannot replace core proof.

rows.decision: Close is an FSM state, not a receipt family.
rows.proof: design + capture; close rejects pane DONE and requires gate refs

rows.decision: Prompt-stack remains POC protocol until FlowMind reaches parity.
rows.proof: prompt-stack prior art; FlowMind replays fixtures before retirement

rows.decision: Receipts are evidence; gates own advancement.
rows.proof: receipt proof spine; no receipt can certify itself

rows.decision: Self-verification is allowed only for low-risk steps; adversarial review is required for high-risk gates.
rows.proof: lev-ralph + proof gate patterns; self mode cannot close high-risk flows

rows.decision: Sessions, receipts, and event buses can be scoped and projected without becoming gate authority.
rows.proof: soft-lineage + session audit; scope refs required and projections cannot advance state

rows.decision: Variables like user.mood are bound from classifier output triples.
rows.proof: FlowMind compiler/interpolation; vars require value, confidence, and evidence_count

rows.decision: Every mutating flow inherits the core run envelope and intent-conservation gate.
rows.proof: core-loop dogfood receipts; overlays cannot replace core proof

props.label: Policy
props.title: Verification Mode Defaults
props.subtitle: Self-scoring is a policy choice, not an accident.

props.content:
intent_conservation:
  category: proof
  scope: core_loop
  default_mode: self_low_risk
  require_review_when:
    - gate_contracts_change
    - dna_gates_change
    - high_risk_close
    - confidence_lt_0_90
  decisions:
    conserved: keep
    unclear: hold
    drifted: mutate
    violated: discard
  consumes:
    - turn_receipt
    - step_receipt
    - gate_receipt
    - trace_ref
    - artifact_ref

props.title: Default stance
props.content: Strong models can self-check low-risk ticks, but Lev should install/recommend reviewer workers for high-risk gates. Model capability becomes a project default, not a hidden runtime assumption.

props.label: Scope
props.title: Scoped Sessions, Receipts, and Event Buses
props.subtitle: The same proof model works for core prompts, SDLC flows, research loops, video editing, and multi-agent stacks.

props.label: Session
props.title: Provider JSONL as source evidence
props.content: Conversation sync should sniff/source provider session stores instead of scraping pane text. tmux stays useful for control, isolation, attach, and injection.

props.label: Bus
props.title: Scoped event buses
props.content: Workstream, session, step, and plugin scopes can all emit events. Events are projections until gates consume them as evidence.

props.label: Overlay
props.title: FlowMind as constraint overlay
props.content: FlowMind can drive linear stacks, semantic FSM branches, nudges, arch interviews, and SDLC lifecycle steps while inheriting the same core proof boundary.

props.label: Roadmap
props.title: Dogfood Sequence
props.subtitle: Start with the core non-SDLC loop, then layer SDLC and non-SDLC FlowMinds.

items.title: Capture and proposal alignment
items.description: Capture the proof spine, produce proposal slices, and keep design fidelity above 0.99.

items.title: Core non-SDLC loop dogfood
items.description: Run prompt-only tasks through plan, act, verify, receipt, gate, and close without FlowMind magic.

items.title: Conversation sync proof
items.description: Bind provider JSONL/session evidence to step receipts and close predicates.

items.title: FlowMind step parity
items.description: Replay prompt-stack fixtures through FlowMind with deterministic step advancement and timeout nudges.

items.title: SDLC overlays
items.description: Apply the same core envelope to entity, design, interview, and dev lifecycle FlowMinds.

items.title: Non-SDLC overlays
items.description: Validate research, media, and arch-interview flows to prove the core loop is substrate-independent.

props.label: Next Routes
props.title: Execution Pack
props.subtitle: These are the immediate implementation tracks implied by the design.

props.label: Proposal
props.title: Core loop proof envelope
props.content: Add intent conservation, gate refs, receipt refs, close state, and verification mode to the core run envelope.

props.label: Proposal
props.title: JSONL conversation sync
props.content: Replace pane scraping and DONE detection with provider session evidence, deterministic idle detection, and scoped step receipts.

props.label: Proposal
props.title: Prompt-stack to FlowMind parity
props.content: Compile prompt stacks into FlowMind steps/FSM conditions, including fan-out, classifier-bound variables, nudges, and close ceremonies.

props.label: Pentagon
props.title: Adversarial probes
props.content: Test false DONE strings, missing gate refs, low-confidence classifier vars, orphan receipts, and high-risk self-close attempts.

props.label: UltraQA
props.title: Runtime QA gauntlet
props.content: Run hostile lifecycle cases across tmux, JSONL sniffing, FlowMind state, receipt emission, and close FSM transitions.

props.label: Docs
props.title: Spec and DNA updates
props.content: Update docs/specs, dna/gates.yaml, and .lev validation-gates references while dna-gates migration is still active.
